meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit for Freelancers: Build Faster & Scale Apps
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps. Use this SaaS starter kit guide with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional, high-converting software for your clients.
content.answer_block: Stop wasting hours on boilerplate code. By leveraging a SaaS starter kit, you unify authentication, payments, and database management into a single, repeatable architecture. Combine this with Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent implementation to transform from a manual coder into a high-velocity AI builder.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, the weight of a new client project pressing down. You’ve built the same login page, the same database schema, and the same Stripe integration ten times before. Every minute spent on these repetitive tasks is a minute you aren't shipping value or scaling your freelance business. You’re trapped in the cycle of basic apps, building the foundation instead of the feature.

But the era of manual boilerplate is over. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you shift your focus from 'how to build' to 'what to build.' This guide provides the blueprint to automate your development lifecycle, turning months of architectural decisions into days of high-impact feature delivery.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Initialize your project with a production-ready SaaS starter kit to ensure security and scalability are baked in from line one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Next.js 14 project structure with Tailwind CSS, Supabase authentication, and Stripe checkout integration. Use a clean, modular architecture suitable for a multi-tenant SaaS application.

steps.title: Configure Database Schemas
steps.description: Define your data models using an ORM to ensure type safety and easy migrations across client projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS. Include tables for users, organizations, subscriptions, and custom project settings with appropriate foreign key relationships.

steps.title: Implement Role-Based Access Control
steps.description: Secure your app by defining strict permissions for owners, admins, and members within your tenant structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware function for Next.js that validates user sessions and checks for organization-level permissions before rendering protected routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file middleware.ts --prompt 'Implement RBAC logic for org-based access'

steps.title: Integrate Payments Pipeline
steps.description: Automate billing cycles and subscription management using Stripe webhooks to handle real-time status updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler to manage subscription status updates: active, past_due, and canceled. Map these to user subscription states in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file api/webhooks/stripe.ts --prompt 'Add Stripe webhook listener for subscription events'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Finalize your environment variables and deploy your starter kit to ensure a seamless hand-off to your client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production deployment checklist for a Next.js SaaS app, including environment variable management, database indexing, and edge function optimization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --command 'npm run build && npm run lint'

pro_tips: Always wrap your database queries in a service layer to prevent tight coupling with your UI components.
Use environment-specific secret management to avoid leaking API keys during local development.
Implement a 'feature flag' system early to allow clients to toggle new modules without redeploying code.
Automate your email notification triggers using an event-driven architecture rather than inline logic.
Standardize your component library in a private repo so you can import UI elements across all your SaaS projects.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is deployed, focus on building a unique 'micro-SaaS' module that solves a specific pain point for your niche. Use the time saved from the boilerplate to iterate on the user experience.
content.conversion_block: Stop building the same foundation over and over. Leverage these tools to reclaim your time, double your output, and deliver enterprise-grade software that keeps clients coming back. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
